rulings.Case Title: MICKEY Y WONG VS. DORIS WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Fourth Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday June 13, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT DORIS WONG MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Fourth Amended Complaint GRANTED. Moving party has shifted its burden. Opposing party failed to address the statute of limitations argument and Corp, Code 16405c, Edwards v. Fresno Community College Hospital (1974) 38 Cal.App.3d 702. Additionally, moving party presented evidence that the partnership, as alleged in the operative complaint, does not exist. (See Wong Deposition Testimony; objection "ambiguous" is overruled; objection "legal conclusion" not raised, and even if raised it would be overruled. The question at issue is different from questions in Rifkind v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 1255). Wong by his declaration describes a different partnership agreement form the one alleged in the operative complaint. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE PAPPAS VS. OFFICE OF THE SAN FRANCISCO SHERIFF ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday June 13, 2016, line 3. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to Plaintiff's Third Causes of Action for violation of 11 U.S.C. § 362(a). The demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to Plaintiff's Second Cause of Action for violation of the Sheriff's internal policies, in order to allow Plaintiff to demonstrate what specific policy was violated. The demurrer is also SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to Plaintiff's Fourth Cause of Action for Intentional Infliction of Emotion Distress to plead a statutory basis for the government's liability. (Searcy v. Hemet Unified School Distr. (1986) 177 Cal.App.3d 792, 802.) Finally, the Demurrer is OVERRULED as to Plaintiff's First Cause of Action for breach of contract. = (501/REQ)
